Ca · 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Mr. Dave Ross 
R&L Consulting Group 
10670 Treena street, 
San Diego, CA 
92131 
Dear Mr. Ross: 
July 15, 1988 
j' I 
suite 210 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-88-225 
You have requested advice on behalf of Mark Loscher, San 
Marcos city Councilmember. 
The advice you seek is under the 
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act").Y 
Your letter indicates that you are authorized by Mr. 
Loscher to request advice on his behalf . 
• 
FACTS 
Mark Loscher is a San Marcos city Councilman. 
He loaned 
his controlled committee approximately $7,000, part of which 
was still outstanding at the time he terminated his committee. 
QUESTION 
May the committee be reactivated to report a repayment of 
the outstanding loan owed to Mr. Loscher? 
CONCLUSION 
The committee may repay the outstanding loan. 
YGovernment Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations section 18000, et~. All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Div sian 6 of the California Code 
ions. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


• 
2 
Mr. Loscher and the committee must file a semi-annual 
campaign statement (Form 490) reporting the repayment and any 
other campaign activity incurred during the period covered by 
the semi-annual statement. 
ANALYSIS 
Officeholders whose salary is SlOP or more per month are 
required to file semi-annual campaign statements. 
(Section 
84200.) 
Any additional activity incurred by an officeholder's 
terminated committee will require that committee to file 
campaign disclosure statements. 
A semi-annual statement will 
be triggered by the terminated committee when the committee 
makes a payment to reduce its outstanding debts. 
(1988 
"Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the 
Political Reform Act," Page 95.) 
Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if 
you have additional questions concerning this matter. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General couns~ 
By: 
vasager 
Reform Consultant

To Whom It May Concern: 
In a recent phone conversation with an FPPC staffer, 
it was conf ir'ined that an individual who had closed a 
recipient 
committee 
with 
a 
stated deficit, 
could 
reopen the same committee in an attempt to repay any

This particular situation involves San Marcos City 
Councilman Mark Loscher, 
who

Committee 
for 
Objective 
Government, 
approximately $7,000, part of which was not repaid. 
The committee was closed showing a deficit, which 
Mr. Loscher had not planned to recover. 
Mr. Loscher has requested that I write this letter as 
his 
campaign 
representative. 
He 
would

a 
wr it ten 
reply, 
stating 
that 
he 
may 
reopen 
his 
committee if the above stated circumstances apply. 
please

If

Thank you for your consideration. 
Group, Inc. 
DR 


• 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
June 17, 1988 
Dave Ross 
R & L Consulting Group 
10670 Treena street, suite 210 
San Diego, CA 
92131 
Re: 
88-225 
Dear Mr. Ross: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on June 16, 1988 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try. to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If more information is needed, the person assigned to 
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to 
advise you as to the information needed. 
If your request is 
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we 
can. 
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. 
Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
/-"::';:""l~. '- / 
// 
/ 'Jeanne Pritchard

and Analysis 
428 J Street, Suite 800 •

Sacramento CA 9')804-0807 •

could 
reopen the same committee in an attempt to repay any 
creditors.

approx ima tely $ 7,000, part of which was not repaid. 
The committee was closed showing a deficit, which 
Mr. Loscher had not planned to recover. 
Mr. Loscher has requested that I write this letter as

Please

If

Thank you for your consideration. 
ulting Group, Inc.
